original_abstract: Learning probabilistic predictive models that are well calibrated is critical for many prediction and decision-making tasks in artificial intelligence. In this paper we present a new non-parametric calibration method called Bayesian Binning into Quantiles (BBQ) which addresses key limitations of existing calibration methods. The method post processes the output of a binary classification algorithm; thus, it can be readily combined with many existing classification algorithms. The method is computationally tractable, and empirically accurate, as evidenced by the set of experiments reported here on both real and simulated datasets.
humanized_abstract: Calibrated probabilistic predictive models are a fundamental component of many AI systems. In this paper we introduce a new non-parametric calibration method called Bayesian Binning into Quantiles (BBQ). This method overcomes the current shortcomings of calibration methods. Our method can be trivially applied to the output of a classifier (post processing the output of a classifier), making it extensible to any current classification method. We demonstrate the efficacy of our method through a series of experiments which show that it is computationally efficient and highly accurate. These experiments were performed on a number of common UCI datasets as well as a number of simulated datasets.
